_meta.author: Necmeddin
_meta.notes: Fixed n/k values at reference wavelength. Dispersion models to be added in v0.2.

SiO2.name: SiO2
references: Malitson, I. H. (1965). J. Opt. Soc. Am. 55(10), 1205.
SiO2.notes: Common AR coating and optical fiber material. Near-zero absorption in visible range.

TiO2.name: TiO2
references: Devore, J. R. (1951). J. Opt. Soc. Am. 41(6), 416.
TiO2.notes: High-index dielectric. Widely used in AR and HR coatings paired with SiO2.

MgF2.name: MgF2
MgF2.notes: Low-index fluoride. Standard single-layer AR coating on glass.

ZnS.name: ZnS
references: Palik, E. D. (1985). Handbook of Optical Constants of Solids. Academic Press.
ZnS.notes: High-index material for visible and IR coatings.

Ta2O5.name: Ta2O5
references: Gilo, M., & Croitoru, N. (1996). Thin Solid Films 283(1-2), 84-89.
Ta2O5.notes: Used in laser optics and bandpass filters. Good thermal and chemical stability.

Al2O3.name: Al2O3
references: Palik, E. D. (1985). Handbook of Optical Constants of Solids. Academic Press.
Al2O3.notes: Hard, durable coating. Used in protective and AR applications.

HfO2.name: HfO2
references: Mehner, A., et al. (2014). Thin Solid Films 574, 1-6.
HfO2.notes: High-index, high-bandgap oxide. Used in UV and high-power laser coatings.

Si3N4.name: Si3N4
Si3N4.notes: PECVD-deposited nitride. Common in photonic integrated circuits and solar cells.

Si.name: Si
references: Green, M. A., & Keevers, M. (1995). Prog. Photovolt. 3(3), 189-192.
Si.notes: Absorbing in visible range. Transparent in near-IR (> 1100 nm).

Ge.name: Ge
references: Palik, E. D. (1985). Handbook of Optical Constants of Solids. Academic Press.
Ge.notes: High index, strongly absorbing in visible. Transparent in mid-IR. Used in IR optics.

Ag.name: Ag
references: Johnson, P. B., & Christy, R. W. (1972). Phys. Rev. B 6(12), 4370.
Ag.notes: High reflectance in visible. Lowest optical losses among metals. Used in mirrors.

Au.name: Au
references: Johnson, P. B., & Christy, R. W. (1972). Phys. Rev. B 6(12), 4370.
Au.notes: High IR reflectance, absorbing in blue-green visible. Used in IR mirrors and plasmonics.

Al.name: Al
references: Palik, E. D. (1985). Handbook of Optical Constants of Solids. Academic Press.
Al.notes: Broad-spectrum reflector. UV-reflective. Common mirror coating.

Cr.name: Cr
references: Palik, E. D. (1985). Handbook of Optical Constants of Solids. Academic Press.
Cr.notes: Highly absorbing. Used as adhesion layer and neutral density filter.

Glass_BK7.name: Glass_BK7
references: Schott AG. (2014). Optical Glass Data Sheet: N-BK7.
Glass_BK7.notes: Standard optical glass. Ubiquitous substrate in optics labs.

Glass_Soda.name: Glass_Soda
references: Standard value — varies slightly by manufacturer.
Glass_Soda.notes: Common window glass. Lower optical quality than BK7.

Quartz.name: Quartz
references: Malitson, I. H. (1965). J. Opt. Soc. Am. 55(10), 1205.
Quartz.notes: UV-transparent substrate. High purity, low OH content versions for deep UV.

_meta.description: Samples measured at Gazi University Photonics Research Center
_meta.status: placeholder — to be populated with experimental data
